headline: Is Philip Morris International (PM) Attractive After 1 Year Return Of 19.7%?
summary: If you are wondering whether Philip Morris International is priced fairly at its current share price, the key question is how that price compares with the value of its future cash flows and assets. The stock recently closed at US$177.93, with a 1 year return of 19.7% and a year to date return of 11.0%, even though it has seen a 4.9% decline over the last 7 days and a 0.8% decline over the last 30 days. Recent coverage has focused on Philip Morris International as a major global tobacco...

headline: Philip Morris Community Donation Adds New Angle To Smoke Free Narrative
summary: Philip Morris International's U.S. businesses have made a significant donation to the California Asian Pacific Chamber of Commerce. The funds are intended to support small business growth and entrepreneurship for socially and economically disadvantaged groups. The initiative is tied to Philip Morris International's broader effort to deepen community partnerships and support minority owned small businesses. For investors watching Philip Morris International, NYSE:PM, this move adds a...

headline: PMI U.S. Invests in Communities; Announces $150,000 Grant to Support California Asian Pacific Chamber of Commerce
summary: Today, Philip Morris International's U.S. businesses ("PMI U.S.") announced a $150,000 donation to the California Asian Pacific Chamber of Commerce (CalAsian Chamber) further demonstrating PMI U.S.'s commitment to investing in communities.

headline: Tobacco-Free Kids: Over 160 Organizations Call on Formula 1 to End All Tobacco Sponsorships and Stop Promoting Addiction to Kids
summary: As the Formula 1 racing season starts this week, over 160 public interest organizations from around the world today sent a letter to Formula 1 calling on the sport to update its prohibition of tobacco product sponsorships to include nicotine pouches and stop helping the tobacco industry market its products to F1's millions of young fans.
